title: lastrea spinulosa var. uliginosa
intro: Lastrea spinulosa var. uliginosa, also known as the marsh fern or uliginous spinulose fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the genus Lastrea and is native to Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of Lastrea spinulosa var. uliginosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant's fronds can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
